corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: In summary, (1) we propose ControlNet, a neural network architecture that can add spatially localized input conditions to a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model via efficient finetuning, (2) we present pretrained ControlNets to control Stable Diffusion, conditioned on Canny edges, Hough lines, user scribbles, human key points, segmentation maps, shape normals, depths, and cartoon line drawings, and (3) we val- idate the method with ablative experiments comparing to several alternative architectures, and conduct user studies focused on several previous baselines across different tasks.

context_window.previous: Finally, we conduct ablative studies to investigate the contribution of each component of our model, and compare our models to several strong conditional image generation baselines with user studies.

Voynov et al. propose an optimization method that fits the diffusion process with sketches.
ControlNet is a neural network architecture that can enhance large pretrained text-to-image diffusion models with spatially localized, task-specific image conditions.
We first introduce the basic structure of a ControlNet in Section 3.1 and then describe how we apply a ControlNet to the image diffusion model Stable Diffusion in Section 3.2.

For instance, the largest datasets for various specific problems (e.g., object shape/normal, human pose extraction, etc.) are usually about 100K in size, which is 50,000 times smaller than the LAION-5B dataset that was used to train Stable Diffusion .

This paper presents ControlNet, an end-to-end neural network architecture that learns conditional controls for large pretrained text-to-image diffusion models (Stable Diffusion in our implementation).
This architecture treats the large pretrained model as a strong backbone for learning diverse conditional controls.

This architecture ensures that harmful noise is not added to the deep features of the large diffusion model at the beginning of training, and protects the large-scale pretrained backbone in the trainable copy from being damaged by such noise.

Additionally, we report that the training of ControlNet is robust and scalable on datasets of different sizes, and that for some tasks like depth-to-image conditioning, training Con-trolNets on a single NVIDIA RTX 3090Ti GPU can achieve results competitive with industrial models trained on large computation clusters.

In summary, we propose ControlNet, a neural network architecture that can add spatially localized input conditions to a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model via efficient finetuning, (2) we present pretrained ControlNets to control Stable Diffusion, conditioned on Canny edges, Hough lines, user scribbles, human key points, segmentation maps, shape normals, depths, and cartoon line drawings, and (3) we validate the method with ablative experiments comparing to several alternative architectures, and conduct user studies focused on several previous baselines across different tasks.

In summary, we propose ControlNet, a neural network architecture that can add spatially localized input conditions to a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model via efficient finetuning, (2) we present pretrained ControlNets to control Stable Diffusion, conditioned on Canny edges, Hough lines, user scribbles, human key points, segmentation maps, shape normals, depths, and cartoon line drawings, and (3) we validate the method with ablative experiments comparing to several alternative architectures, and conduct user studies focused on several previous baselines across different tasks.

In summary, we propose ControlNet, a neural network architecture that can add spatially localized input conditions to a pretrained text-to-image diffusion model via efficient finetuning, (2) we present pretrained ControlNets to control Stable Diffusion, conditioned on Canny edges, Hough lines, user scribbles, human key points, segmentation maps, shape normals, depths, and cartoon line drawings, and (3) we validate the method with ablative experiments comparing to several alternative architectures, and conduct user studies focused on several previous baselines across different tasks.

Specifically, we show the results of PITI , Sketch-Guided Diffusion , and Taming Transformers . (Note that the backbone of PITI is OpenAI GLIDE that have different visual quality and performance.) We observe that ControlNet can robustly handle diverse conditioning images and achieves sharp and clean results.
